# Veryfront Framework Documentation

## Overview
Veryfront is a modern React framework for Deno with multi-runtime support (Deno, Node.js, Bun, Cloudflare Workers), flexible rendering modes (SSR, SSG, ISR, JIT, RSC), and native AI agent capabilities.

## Core Documentation

### Getting Started (Priority: High)
- /docs/introduction.md: What is Veryfront and why use it - comprehensive introduction to the framework, its philosophy, and key differentiators
- /docs/quick-start.md: 5-minute quickstart guide for both traditional React apps and AI-native applications with complete working examples
- /docs/getting-started/installation.md: Installation instructions for Deno, Node.js, Bun, and Cloudflare Workers with runtime-specific configuration

### Routing (Priority: High)
- /docs/routing/README.md: Complete overview of Veryfront's file-based routing system supporting both App Router and Pages Router patterns
- /docs/routing/app-router.md: App Router guide (Next.js 13+ style) with nested layouts, route groups, and parallel routes
- /docs/routing/pages-router.md: Pages Router guide (Next.js 12 style) with file-based routing conventions
- /docs/routing/dynamic-routes.md: Dynamic route segments including [slug], [...slug] catch-all, and [[...slug]] optional catch-all patterns
- /docs/routing/api-routes.md: Creating API endpoints with route.ts (App Router) and pages/api/ (Pages Router) patterns

### Rendering (Priority: High)
- /docs/rendering/README.md: Complete overview of all rendering modes - SSR, SSG, ISR, JIT, and RSC with use cases for each
- /docs/rendering/comparison.md: Decision matrix and comparison table for choosing the right rendering mode for your application
- /docs/rendering/ssr.md: Server-Side Rendering implementation with data fetching and caching strategies
- /docs/rendering/ssg.md: Static Site Generation with getStaticPaths for pre-rendering at build time
- /docs/rendering/isr.md: Incremental Static Regeneration for updating static pages without full rebuilds
- /docs/rendering/jit.md: Just-In-Time rendering - Veryfront's unique rendering mode for large-scale sites with on-demand generation

### AI System (Priority: High)
- /docs/ai/README.md: Complete AI documentation index with overview of all AI capabilities and integration patterns
- /docs/ai/getting-started.md: Build AI-powered applications in 5 minutes with agents, tools, and context management
- /docs/reference/ai/agent.md: Agent API reference with configuration options
- /docs/reference/ai/tools.md: Tool definition and auto-discovery system
- /docs/reference/ai/hooks.md: React hooks for AI features (useChat, useAgent)

### API Reference (Priority: Medium)
- /docs/api/README.md: Complete API surface documentation with all public APIs, types, and interfaces
- /docs/api/configuration.md: veryfront.config.ts complete reference with all configuration options, defaults, and runtime-specific settings
- /docs/api/components.md: Built-in components including Link, Head, OptimizedImage with props and usage examples
- /docs/api/data-fetching.md: Data fetching APIs including getServerData, getStaticPaths, notFound, and redirect functions
- /docs/api/routes.md: API route handlers with HTTP method support (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH) and request/response handling

### Deployment (Priority: Medium)
- /docs/guides/deployment/deno.md: Complete deployment guide for Deno Deploy with configuration, environment variables, and best practices
- /docs/guides/deployment/node.md: Deploy to Node.js environments (Vercel, Railway, DigitalOcean) with adapter configuration
- /docs/guides/deployment/bun.md: Deploy to Bun runtime with performance optimizations and configuration
- /docs/guides/deployment/cloudflare.md: Deploy to Cloudflare Workers with edge runtime considerations and limitations

### Guides (Priority: Medium)
- /docs/guides/README.md: Guides index with categorized tutorials and how-to articles
- /docs/guides/building-blog.md: Step-by-step tutorial for building a complete blog with dynamic routes, MDX, and styling
- /docs/guides/data-fetching-patterns.md: Common data fetching patterns for different scenarios (real-time, cached, static, client-side)
- /docs/guides/image-optimization.md: Image optimization best practices using OptimizedImage component with format conversion and lazy loading
- /docs/guides/performance.md: Performance optimization techniques including code splitting, lazy loading, and caching strategies
- /docs/guides/styling.md: Styling approaches including CSS modules, Tailwind CSS, CSS-in-JS, and global styles

### Platform Adapters (Priority: Low)
- /docs/platform-adapters/overview.md: Overview of multi-runtime architecture and adapter system
- /docs/platform-adapters/deno.md: Deno adapter with native Deno APIs and deploy integration
- /docs/platform-adapters/nodejs.md: Node.js adapter with compatibility layer and npm package support
- /docs/platform-adapters/bun.md: Bun adapter with performance optimizations and native APIs
- /docs/platform-adapters/cloudflare.md: Cloudflare Workers adapter with edge runtime specifics

### Advanced Topics (Priority: Low)
- /docs/architecture.md: Detailed architecture overview covering rendering pipeline, routing system, and build process
- /docs/debugging.md: Comprehensive debugging guide with common issues, error messages, and troubleshooting steps

### Examples (Priority: Medium)
19 working example applications available in /examples/ directory:

**AI Examples** (8 examples):
- /examples/ai-basic/: Basic AI agent integration showing minimal setup
- /examples/ai-code-assistant/: Complete AI-powered code assistant with tool integration
- /examples/full-demo/: Comprehensive demo showcasing all Veryfront features including AI
- /examples/knowledge-base/: Knowledge base application with AI-powered search and Q&A
- /examples/ai-autodiscovery/: AI agent autodiscovery and dynamic tool registration
- /examples/ai-phase3/: Advanced AI features including streaming and context management
- /examples/ai-dev-tools/: Developer tools powered by AI agents
- /examples/ai-sdk-integration/: Integration with external AI SDKs

**Core Examples** (9 examples):
- /examples/minimal-app-router/: Minimal App Router setup - great starting point
- /examples/minimal-pages/: Minimal Pages Router setup
- /examples/auth-app/: Complete authentication example with protected routes
- /examples/data-fetching-demo/: Data fetching patterns for SSR, SSG, and client-side
- /examples/basic-mdx/: MDX integration for content-rich applications
- /examples/form-handling/: Form handling with validation and server actions
- /examples/middleware-demo/: Request/response middleware patterns
- /examples/rsc-demo/: React Server Components demonstration

**Infrastructure** (2 examples):
- /examples/async-worker-redis/: Background job processing with Redis
- /examples/coding-agent/: Autonomous coding agent (bridges AI and infrastructure)

## Key Concepts

### Rendering Modes
- **SSR** (Server-Side Rendering): Render on each request for dynamic content
- **SSG** (Static Site Generation): Pre-render at build time for static content
- **ISR** (Incremental Static Regeneration): Update static pages periodically without full rebuild
- **JIT** (Just-In-Time): Veryfront's unique mode - generate static pages on first request
- **RSC** (React Server Components): Experimental support for React Server Components

### Routing Patterns
- **App Router**: Next.js 13+ style with layouts, loading states, and nested routes (app/ directory)
- **Pages Router**: Next.js 12 style with simpler file-based routing (pages/ directory)
- **Dynamic Routes**: [param], [...params], [[...params]] for variable URL segments
- **API Routes**: Server-side API endpoints with HTTP method handlers

### Data Fetching
- **getServerData**: Fetch data on server for SSR and SSG
- **getStaticPaths**: Generate static paths for dynamic routes
- **Client-side**: Use any data fetching library (fetch, axios, SWR, React Query)

### AI Capabilities
- **Native Agents**: Built-in AI agent system with tool calling and context management
- **Multiple Providers**: Support for Anthropic, OpenAI, Google AI, and custom providers
- **Tool System**: Extensible tool system for agent actions with schema validation
- **MCP Integration**: Model Context Protocol for standardized AI interactions
- **Production Ready**: Rate limiting, caching, observability, and error handling

## Multi-Runtime Support
Veryfront runs on:
- **Deno** (primary runtime): Native TypeScript, secure by default
- **Node.js**: Full npm ecosystem compatibility
- **Bun**: High-performance JavaScript runtime
- **Cloudflare Workers**: Edge computing platform

## Version Information
- Current version: 0.1.0 (pre-release)
- Status: Active development
- License: MIT
- Repository: Internal monorepo

## Documentation Format
All documentation includes:
- YAML frontmatter with metadata for AI agents
- Complete, runnable code examples
- TypeScript type definitions
- Use cases and best practices
- Common errors and troubleshooting
- Related documentation links

## For AI Agents
This documentation is structured for both human developers and AI agents:
- **Consistent patterns**: Predictable structure and terminology throughout
- **Complete examples**: All code examples include imports, types, and context
- **Explicit types**: TypeScript signatures for all APIs
- **Structured metadata**: YAML frontmatter with category, level, keywords
- **Clear relationships**: Related documentation clearly linked

## Getting Help
- GitHub Issues: Report bugs and request features
- Examples: 19 working examples covering all use cases
- Quick Start: Get running in 5 minutes with /docs/quick-start.md
- AI Documentation: Comprehensive AI capabilities in /docs/ai/

## Quick Links for Common Tasks

**Starting a new project:**
1. Read /docs/quick-start.md
2. Choose router style: /docs/routing/app-router.md or /docs/routing/pages-router.md
3. Choose rendering mode: /docs/rendering/comparison.md
4. Deploy: /docs/guides/deployment/deno.md

**Building with AI:**
1. Start with /docs/ai/getting-started.md
2. Review /docs/reference/ai/agent.md and /docs/reference/ai/tools.md for API reference
3. See working examples: /examples/ai-code-assistant/ or /examples/knowledge-base/

**Migrating from Next.js:**
1. Veryfront supports both App Router and Pages Router patterns
2. Most Next.js code works with minimal changes
3. See /examples/minimal-app-router/ for familiar patterns

**Adding dynamic routes:**
1. Read /docs/routing/dynamic-routes.md
2. Use [slug] for single params, [...slug] for catch-all
3. Implement getServerData for data fetching

**Optimizing performance:**
1. Review /docs/guides/performance.md
2. Choose appropriate rendering mode from /docs/rendering/comparison.md
3. Use OptimizedImage component: /docs/api/components.md
